visit: How about this? (She flashes a bundle of cash.)
accept: You love wealth far too much. Your heart looks only to riches, when it should look to God.
reject: Your riches will rot, your clothes will decay, your gold will rust… and in the end, misfortune will be all that remains.
death: I can’t see the moon anymore…

visit: I’ve lost my way… could you let me stay just one night?
accept: Thank you, dear… you saved me.
reject: I see… can’t be helped. May you be cursed!
death: Ahh… why did it have to end like this…

visit: Honesty and kindness… that’s what the world needs right now.
accept: You helped me… I’m grateful, truly.
reject: Not every night is bright with moonlight, you know.
death: No more movies… no more books… no more seeing that person again…

visit: Interested in knowing where the treasure is?
accept: Treasure…? Oh my, whatever do you mean…
reject: I see… well, that’s a shame, but it can’t be helped.
death: So this is… what death smells like…

visit: This village is so quiet and lovely… mind letting me in?
accept: Thank you, dear… that makes me happy.
reject: There’s nowhere else left for me to go…
death: How much would I have to pay… for you to save me?

visit: Think you could let an old lady in~?
reject: I see… sorry for the trouble, dear.

visit: The road ahead was dangerous… could you let me stay here tonight?
accept: A single cup of coffee… that alone can give someone a reason to live.
reject: Strange thing to do… turning me away like that.
death: Ever think this whole world might just be someone’s game?

visit: Nights get cold, you know… mind if I rest here?
accept: Thank you, dear… you helped me a lot.
reject: That’s all you have to say?

visit: I’m a traveling merchant… could you let me stay for one night?
accept: Dried meat… want some? What kind? Oh ho ho… wouldn’t you like to know.
reject: You’re not letting me in? Really now… is that your final answer?
death: In the end… I’m thinking of that person…

visit: You letting me in? Or not? Which is it!
accept: You’ve got a good eye, you know.
reject: You stupid, useless fool! *Peh, peh, peh!* I refuse YOU!
